title: sys: addaudithook lazily creates interp->audit_hooks with no lock, racing should_audit on every audit event
description: sys_addaudithook_impl lazily initializes the per-interpreter audit-hook list (interp->audit_hooks = PyList_New(0)) with a plain unlocked pointer store; should_audit reads that same pointer on every audit event. The first-ever sys.addaudithook() store races with concurrent audit reads and sibling addaudithook stores. Unlike the C-level audit list (guarded by runtime->audit_hooks.mutex), the Python-level list has no synchronization, so concurrent first-time init can also silently drop a hook (security-relevant PEP 578 correctness bug).
sites: Python/sysmodule.c sys_addaudithook_impl:540 (WRITE: interp->audit_hooks = PyList_New(0), unlocked lazy init)
Python/sysmodule.c should_audit:239 (READ: || interp->audit_hooks, on every audit event via sys_audit_tstate)
race_kind: unsynchronized lazy-init of interpreter-global state (plain pointer store vs plain load; no lock/atomic)
shared_state: PyInterpreterState.audit_hooks (per-interpreter Python-level audit-hook list; for the main interpreter it lives inside the _PyRuntime global)
severity: low-to-moderate (flagged pointer race is value-benign on aligned access, but the same missing lock allows a silently-dropped audit hook (security-relevant correctness) and a latent append-vs-iterate list-internals race)
repro_reliability: deterministic (exit 66, 3/3 runs); write@540 is one-shot per process so the repro collides many first-time addaudithook stores behind a barrier while other threads spin sys.audit()
suggested_fix: serialize interp->audit_hooks create+append under the existing runtime->audit_hooks.mutex and use FT_ATOMIC_LOAD/STORE_PTR_RELAXED on the pointer (read in should_audit, NULL-check + store in sys_addaudithook_impl); snapshot the list before iterating in sys_audit_tstate so append can't realloc under a live iterator
notes: Genuine interpreter-global-state race, not a 'don't share this object' scenario -- there is no per-object alternative. The C-level global hook list is already mutex-guarded (runtime->audit_hooks.mutex, locked by PySys_AddAuditHook:507), but the Python-level interp->audit_hooks list added by sys_addaudithook_impl got no equivalent guard -- looks like an incomplete free-threading migration. The in-code 'benign race' comments (sysmodule.c:292-295, 439-442) reason about the C-level singly-linked list, not this pointer-store lazy init. No network access to check the upstream tracker; cross-reference audit-subsystem FT discussions before filing.

related_issues.note: addaudithook exception-check bug (PR #152913) — DIFFERENT (exception handling, not the data race)

assessment: real, new — unlocked lazy init of interp->audit_hooks; C-level list already mutex-guarded (incomplete FT migration); security-relevant
